clusters.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
clusters.neutral_headline: Last passengers leave virus-hit cruise ship as three more test positive
bullet_summary: An American citizen and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive, officials said, as the repatriation operation continued on Monday
So far, at least 6 cases have been confirmed, two are suspected and three passengers have died following the outbreak of the rare virus – for which no specific cure exists
A French passenger who was on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for the virus and her condition is deteriorating, French authorities said
President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage

items.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
items.standfirst: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran to make a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.
items.content: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran to make a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.

items.content: An American and a French national who have returned home have tested positive for hantavirus.

items.title: Middle East war live: France, UK host Hormuz meeting as Trump says Iran truce ‘on life support’
items.standfirst: French Defence Minister Catherine Vautrin and her British counterpart John Healey will co-chair a multinational meeting Tuesday on military plans to restore trade flows through the blocked Strait of Hormuz after the Iran war ends. The virtual meeting of dozens of defence ministers comes as President Donald Trump warned the US-Iran ceasefire was on “life support”. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: French Defence Minister Catherine Vautrin and her British counterpart John Healey will co-chair a multinational meeting Tuesday on military plans to restore trade flows through the blocked Strait of Hormuz after the Iran war ends. The virtual meeting of dozens of defence ministers comes as President Donald Trump warned the US-Iran ceasefire was on “life support”. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Last passengers from hantavirus-hit ship evacuated; American tests positive

items.content: US officials say 18 Americans are being monitored for symptoms, as WHO insists risk to public remains low.

items.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
items.standfirst: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".
items.content: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

items.title: Mass starvation looms if fertilisers can’t pass key waterway, UN warns – as it happened
items.standfirst: This blog is now closed. See full report: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire fraysTurkey’s foreign minister, Hakan Fidan, will visit Qatar later today for talks on the war, its impact on the region and efforts to ensure navigational safety in the strait of Hormuz is resumed, a Turkish diplomatic source told the Reuters news agency.Turkey, which neighbours Iran, has been in close contact with the US, Iran and mediator Pakistan since the start of the conflict. It condemnded the US and Israel for launching the war, widely seen to have been done illegally, but also criticised Iran’s counter strikes on Gulf states. Continue reading...
items.content: This blog is now closed. See full report: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire fraysTurkey’s foreign minister, Hakan Fidan, will visit Qatar later today for talks on the war, its impact on the region and efforts to ensure navigational safety in the strait of Hormuz is resumed, a Turkish diplomatic source told the Reuters news agency.Turkey, which neighbours Iran, has been in close contact with the US, Iran and mediator Pakistan since the start of the conflict. It condemnded the US and Israel for launching the war, widely seen to have been done illegally, but also criticised Iran’s counter strikes on Gulf states. Continue reading...

items.title: Americans from hantavirus-hit ship arrive in U.S., including 1 who tested positive
items.standfirst: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.
items.content: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

items.title: Why Americans from the hantavirus-stricken ship were taken to Omaha
items.standfirst: Most of the Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak were taken to specialized facilities at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.
items.content: Most of the Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak were taken to specialized facilities at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.

items.title: Americans enter quarantine after returning from hantavirus cruise ship
items.standfirst: The 18 Americans who were aboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship have returned to the U.S. and are now in quarantine. One remains in the biocontainment unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center after testing positive. Ian Lee has more.
items.content: The 18 Americans who were aboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship have returned to the U.S. and are now in quarantine. One remains in the biocontainment unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center after testing positive. Ian Lee has more.

items.title: US passengers quarantining in Nebraska as officials say public hantavirus risk 'very low'
items.standfirst: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, 16 in Nebraska and another six passengers in Canada.
items.content: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, 16 in Nebraska and another six passengers in Canada.

items.content: Americans enter quarantine after returning from hantavirus cruise ship; President Trump aims to suspend gas tax amid high prices due to war with Iran.

items.title: Why cruise ship passengers with possible hantavirus exposure went to Nebraska
items.standfirst: The University of Nebraska is home to the only federally funded quarantine unit in the U.S. and a separate biocontainment unit that can treat people exposed to infectious diseases.
items.content: The University of Nebraska is home to the only federally funded quarantine unit in the U.S. and a separate biocontainment unit that can treat people exposed to infectious diseases.

items.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran on ‘life support’ after rejecting peace proposals
items.standfirst: US president says he is considering restarting naval escorts in strait of Hormuz in attempt to end Iranian blockadeDonald Trump has said the ceasefire with Iran is on “life support” and that he is considering restarting US navy military escorts of ships through the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to end the Iranian blockade of the vital waterway.The US president dismissed Iran’s peace proposals as stupid, and denied he was under any domestic pressure to reach a deal. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says he is considering restarting naval escorts in strait of Hormuz in attempt to end Iranian blockadeDonald Trump has said the ceasefire with Iran is on “life support” and that he is considering restarting US navy military escorts of ships through the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to end the Iranian blockade of the vital waterway.The US president dismissed Iran’s peace proposals as stupid, and denied he was under any domestic pressure to reach a deal. Continue reading...

items.title: Hantavirus outbreak: Should we worry about the virus spreading?
items.standfirst: An American citizen and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive, officials said, as the repatriation operation continued on Monday. So far, at least 6 cases have been confirmed, two are suspected and three passengers have died following the outbreak of the rare virus – for which no specific cure exists. But should we worry about the virus spreading?
items.content: An American citizen and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive, officials said, as the repatriation operation continued on Monday. So far, at least 6 cases have been confirmed, two are suspected and three passengers have died following the outbreak of the rare virus – for which no specific cure exists. But should we worry about the virus spreading?

items.title: Hantavirus: France enforces strict measures after citizen tests positive
items.standfirst: One person in France has tested positive for Hantavirus. This comes after five people from the cruise ship were repatriated on Sunday after the vessel docked in the Spanish port of Tenerife. According to the French minister the patient's condition worsened overnight.
items.content: One person in France has tested positive for Hantavirus. This comes after five people from the cruise ship were repatriated on Sunday after the vessel docked in the Spanish port of Tenerife. According to the French minister the patient's condition worsened overnight.

items.title: Hantavirus: French PM Lecornu holds talks with medical advisers, ministers
items.standfirst: A French passenger who was on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for the virus and her condition is deteriorating, French authorities said. France's Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu will hold a meeting on the hantavirus crisis on Monday. FRANCE 24's James André reports from Paris, France.
items.content: A French passenger who was on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for the virus and her condition is deteriorating, French authorities said. France's Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu will hold a meeting on the hantavirus crisis on Monday. FRANCE 24's James André reports from Paris, France.

items.title: Trump says ceasefire is ‘on life support’ after rejecting ‘unacceptable’ proposal from Iran
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage.”
items.content: President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage.”

items.title: Oil prices climb after Trump dismisses Iran’s response to peace plan
items.standfirst: Brent crude rises after US president calls overture from Tehran ‘totally unacceptable’Oil prices have climbed after Donald Trump condemned Iran’s response to US proposals to end the war as “totally unacceptable”.The president’s rejection of Tehran’s overture in a post on his Truth Social platform triggered a jump in Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil prices, by as much as 4% on Monday to $105.50 a barrel, before easing back to settle at $103.50. Continue reading...
items.content: Brent crude rises after US president calls overture from Tehran ‘totally unacceptable’Oil prices have climbed after Donald Trump condemned Iran’s response to US proposals to end the war as “totally unacceptable”.The president’s rejection of Tehran’s overture in a post on his Truth Social platform triggered a jump in Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil prices, by as much as 4% on Monday to $105.50 a barrel, before easing back to settle at $103.50. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says Iran's response to peace proposal "totally unacceptable"
items.standfirst: President Trump didn't provide details on the issues he had with the response or what would come next.
items.content: President Trump didn't provide details on the issues he had with the response or what would come next.

items.content: The 18 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius arrived back in the U.S. on Monday, and two of them are in biocontainment units in Omaha, Nebraska, and Atlanta

items.title: Emerging Currencies Drop as Iran Conflict Dims Risk Appetite
items.standfirst: Emerging-market currencies weakened against the dollar on Monday as oil advanced and tensions in the Middle East escalated, with President Donald Trump and Iran’s leadership rejecting each other’s peace proposals and signaling no end in sight to the 10-week-old war.
items.content: Emerging-market currencies weakened against the dollar on Monday as oil advanced and tensions in the Middle East escalated, with President Donald Trump and Iran’s leadership rejecting each other’s peace proposals and signaling no end in sight to the 10-week-old war.

items.content: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship headed back to the United States. Trump says Iran proposal "totally unacceptable."

items.title: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
items.standfirst: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.
items.content: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

items.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire frays
items.standfirst: US president expresses ire at Tehran’s reported demands, as drones strike Gulf nations and Israel warns war ‘not over’Donald Trump has rejected an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable”, on a day the month-old ceasefire showed signs of fraying as drone strikes were reported around the region and Benjamin Netanyahu warned the war was “not over”.The Iranian counter-proposal was passed to Washington through Pakistani mediators. Continue reading...
items.content: US president expresses ire at Tehran’s reported demands, as drones strike Gulf nations and Israel warns war ‘not over’Donald Trump has rejected an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable”, on a day the month-old ceasefire showed signs of fraying as drone strikes were reported around the region and Benjamin Netanyahu warned the war was “not over”.The Iranian counter-proposal was passed to Washington through Pakistani mediators. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon.
items.content: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon.

items.title: White House press gala shooting suspect pleads not guilty to attempted assassination of Donald Trump
items.standfirst: A California man accused of attempting to assassinate US President Donald Trump at last month's White House Correspondents' Dinner pleaded not guilty to all charges on Monday. Prosecutors allege Cole Allen fired ⁠a shotgun at a US Secret Service agent and stormed a security checkpoint in a foiled attack in April.
items.content: A California man accused of attempting to assassinate US President Donald Trump at last month's White House Correspondents' Dinner pleaded not guilty to all charges on Monday. Prosecutors allege Cole Allen fired ⁠a shotgun at a US Secret Service agent and stormed a security checkpoint in a foiled attack in April.

items.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect Cole Allen pleads not guilty
items.standfirst: Allen is charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump, assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two gun counts.
items.content: Allen is charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump, assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two gun counts.

items.title: Abortion pill by mail allowed for at least 3 more days, the Supreme Court says
items.standfirst: Full access to the abortion pill mifepristone, including through telemedicine and the mail, will continue for at least three more days, the high court said on Monday.
items.content: Full access to the abortion pill mifepristone, including through telemedicine and the mail, will continue for at least three more days, the high court said on Monday. (Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)

items.title: Supreme Court continues to temporarily maintain mail access to abortion pill
items.standfirst: Justice Samuel Alito extended an administrative stay that maintained access to mifepristone through the mail.
items.content: Justice Samuel Alito extended an administrative stay that maintained access to mifepristone through the mail.

6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement

items.title: Remains of US soldier who fell off cliff in Morocco found in ocean
items.standfirst: The remains of a US soldier who went missing during training exercises in Morocco have been recovered in the Atlantic Ocean.
items.content: The remains of a US soldier who went missing during training exercises in Morocco have been recovered in the Atlantic Ocean.

items.title: Body of missing US soldier recovered off coast of Morocco
items.standfirst: 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr was participating in military exercise among US, Nato allies and African countriesA search team recovered the body of a US soldier who went missing near a cliff during a training exercise in Cap Draa, Morocco, the US army said on Sunday.Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1.6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement.
